hypothesis: If a generative model is pre-trained on large chemical databases (ChEMBL/ZINC), fine-tuned on a curated Covalent Triazine Framework dataset, and optimized with reinforcement learning using expert-defined rewards (flatness, symmetry, conjugation, steric hindrance, pattern matching), then the resulting CTF structures will show higher validity, uniqueness, internal diversity, and novelty, lower Fréchet ChemNet Distance, and a greater rate of passing expert filters than configurations without this transfer-learning and reward-design approach.
